title: digitalis purpurea var. parviflora
intro: Digitalis purpurea var. parviflora, also known as the small-flowered foxglove, is a variety of the foxglove plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It contains cardiac glycosides, specifically digoxin and digitoxin, which have been used in herbal medicine to treat various heart conditions, including atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The medicinal properties of Digitalis purpurea var. parviflora have been shown to help regulate heart rate and improve cardiac function, leading to improved overall health and reduced risk of heart-related complications. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are typically dried and used to make tea or tinctures, which can be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
